body:
Would this be GPU compatible? And can I make allocations go all the way to zero?

It is definitely faster! Showing benchmarks for Float32:

```
NonlinearSolver version (current main):

BenchmarkTools.Trial: 4726 samples with 1 evaluation.
 Range (min … max):  1.004 ms …   2.797 ms  ┊ GC (min … max): 0.00% … 62.61%
 Time  (median):     1.022 ms               ┊ GC (median):    0.00%
 Time  (mean ± σ):   1.057 ms ± 204.686 μs  ┊ GC (mean ± σ):  2.91% ±  8.38%
 Memory estimate: 1.02 MiB, allocs estimate: 11294.

RootSolvers version (PR):

BenchmarkTools.Trial: 10000 samples with 10 evaluations.
 Range (min … max):  1.321 μs … 105.704 μs  ┊ GC (min … max): 0.00% … 97.07%
 Time  (median):     1.350 μs               ┊ GC (median):    0.00%
 Time  (mean ± σ):   1.426 μs ±   1.765 μs  ┊ GC (mean ± σ):  2.11% ±  1.69%
 Memory estimate: 1.45 KiB, allocs estimate: 71.
```

Also, are [Integrals.jl](https://docs.sciml.ai/Integrals/stable/) ok to use on GPU?
title: Use RootSolvers in P3
